source: Pre-Training Tasks for Embedding-Based Large-Scale Retrieval

concepts.finding: Pre-training tasks for dual-encoder retrieval models: (1) Inverse Cloze Task (predict context given sentence), (2) Sentence prediction (predict next sentence), (3) Masked LM term prediction. ICT provides strongest signal for retrieval. Pre-training + fine-tuning on MS MARCO achieves SOTA on recall. Key insight: pre-training tasks designed specifically for retrieval (not just language modeling) matter.
concepts.implication: For keyword embedding pre-training: design tasks that predict keyword context (co-occurring terms, search sessions, clicked documents) rather than general language modeling. ICT-style: given keyword, predict its co-occurrence context in search sessions.
concepts.open_question: What pre-training tasks best capture keyword-intent relationships? Is ICT on search sessions more effective than on documents?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Keyword embeddings pre-trained with retrieval-specific tasks (predicting co-occurring queries in sessions) outperform LM-pre-trained embeddings for keyword-intent clustering.
